summary: Large partial decompilation of the item common module around `it_266F`, adding roughly 1.1k lines of C plus headers and supporting type/signature fixes. The PR implemented many item spawning, item collision/landing, bone/JObj display, and helper routines, while updating shared item structs and call sites so newly typed functions could be used from existing item files. No PR body, issue comments, reviews, or review comments were present in the slice, so intent beyond the title and diff is inferred from code changes.
what_changed: Expanded `src/melee/it/it_266F.c` from a tiny stub area into a large partial implementation of the `it_266F` item-common region, adding functions such as `it_8026C47C`, `it_8026C65C`, `it_8026C704`, `it_8026C75C`, `fn_8026C88C`, `it_8026CA4C`, `it_8026CB3C`, `it_8026CB9C`, `it_8026CF04`, `it_8026D018`, `it_8026D324`, `it_8026D3CC`, many `it_8026D5xx`/`it_8026DExx` collision helpers, `it_8026EB18`, `it_8026EBC8`, `it_8026EC54`, `it_8026ECE0`, `it_8026EECC`, `it_8026F3AC`, and `it_8026C530`.

Added many includes to `it_266F.c` for camera, debug, effects, fighter library, game mode, ground/stage, item helpers, collision, mp library, baselib object/proc/JObj/memory/random/state/TEV, and math dependencies.
Expanded `it_266F.h` with an `ItCallback` typedef and many address-commented prototypes for the newly implemented functions.

Corrected existing prototypes in `it_266F.h`, including making `it_8026D324` take `s32`, changing `it_8026DA70` to return `bool` and take `Item_GObj*`, and replacing some no-argument collision helper prototypes with `Item_GObj*`/`HSD_GObj*`-based signatures.
Added definitions for item allocation globals in `it_2725.c`: `it_804A0E30`, `it_804A0E50`, and `it_804A0E60`, with `it_804A0E50` now typed as `HSD_ObjAllocUnk6`.
Added item helper prototypes to `it_2725.h`, including `it_80272828`, `it_802734B4`, `it_80275DFC`, `it_802762D8`, `it_80276CEC`, `it_80276FC4`, and `it_80277544`.
Updated `item.c` initialization to access nested fields of the new `HSD_ObjAllocUnk4` layout, changing direct `it_804A0E30.xC`/`x4` writes to `it_804A0E30.x4.x8` and `it_804A0E30.x4.x0`.

Updated item call sites in `it_2ADA.c`, `itfflower.c`, `itfflowerflame.c`, and `ithammer.c` to pass `gobj` into `it_8026DF34`/`it_8026DFB0` after those helpers were typed as object-dependent routines.
Adjusted `itlinkarrow.c` to use `Vec3*` instead of `UNK_T` for a local pointer-like collision/output value and passed an existing `Vec3` address into `it_8026EA20`.

Refined `src/melee/it/types.h`: `ItemAttr.x58` and `x5c` became `f32`, `Item.xAC8_hurtboxNum` and `Item.xD50_landNum` became `u32`, `ItemCommonData` gained explicit `x128`, `x12C`, `x130`, and `x134` fields, and a new `itUnkVars` union member was added under `Item.xDD4_itemVar.unk`.

Restructured placeholder object allocation structs in `types.h`: `HSD_ObjAllocUnk6` became a compact weighted-list-like structure with `x0`, `x4`, `x8`, `xA`, and `xC`, while `HSD_ObjAllocUnk4` became a wrapper containing `x0`, nested `HSD_ObjAllocUnk6 x4`, `x14`, and `u64 x18`.
Added or corrected supporting external prototypes: `db_80225D54`, `it_804A0E30`, `gm_8016AE94`, `gm_8016AEA4`, `lbColl_8000A10C`, `mpColl_8004B2DC`, `mpColl_8004D024`, and `mpLib_800524DC`.
smart_moves: The PR did not only add C for `it_266F`; it propagated signature corrections through real callers, which reduces reliance on `UNK_PARAMS` and makes later item decompilation safer.

Several behavior helpers were typed around `Item_GObj*`, `HSD_GObj*`, `Item*`, `CollData*`, and `ItCallback`, matching recurring item-state patterns instead of leaving generic `void*`/`UNK_T` interfaces.
The `HSD_ObjAllocUnk4`/`HSD_ObjAllocUnk6` split captured observed nested layout usage, enabling code like `it_804A0E30.x4.x8` and weighted item-list routines to typecheck cleanly.
The diff preserved uncertainty with `@todo` comments and an `#if 0` block for an unready function, rather than forcing a speculative match into live code.

The implementation reused known project idioms such as `GET_ITEM(gobj)`, `CollData* coll = &ip->x378_itemColl`, address-commented prototypes, and offset-based field names, keeping the partial match compatible with existing decomp style.
decomp_lessons: When a helper originally appears argumentless but reads item state, check call sites and assembly for a hidden `gobj` argument; this PR updated multiple item files to pass `gobj` to `it_8026DF34` and `it_8026DFB0`.
Struct-field type fixes can unlock matches: changing item attributes at offsets `0x58`/`0x5c` to `f32` aligns with `fabs_inline` comparisons in `it_8026DC24`.

Unsigned counters matter for matching and comparisons: `xAC8_hurtboxNum` and `xD50_landNum` were changed to `u32`, which matches code that compares landing counts and hurtbox counts as unsigned values.

For common item collision routines, the repeated sequence is `it_80276214(gobj)`, run an `mpColl_*` test, copy `coll->cur_topn` into `ip->pos`, and update `ip->xC30` from `coll->floor.index` when the test succeeds.

Weighted random selection routines may require modeling allocator/global state, not just local function bodies; `it_8026C65C`, `it_8026C75C`, `it_8026CB9C`, and `it_8026C530` all rely on the newly shaped `HSD_ObjAllocUnk6` fields.

If a decomp block depends on shared globals, add exact externs/definitions in the owning module and headers at the same time; this PR paired `db_2253.h`, `it_2725.c`, `it_266F.h`, and `types.h` changes.
Leaving a known-unmatched routine under `#if 0` with stack/local evidence can be useful documentation, but it should not be treated as a completed match.
naming_conventions: New prototypes follow the project’s address-comment convention, e.g. `/* 26D324 */ bool it_8026D324(s32);`.
Object parameters are increasingly named `gobj` when typed, replacing generic `arg0` in nearby item code such as `itfflowerflame.c`.
Item object typedefs distinguish `Item_GObj*` from generic `HSD_GObj*`; callbacks were normalized through `typedef void (*ItCallback)(HSD_GObj *)` in `it_266F.h`.
Unknown fields retain offset-style names such as `xD50_landNum`, `xC30`, `x378_itemColl`, `xDD4_itemVar`, and `x128`/`x12C`/`x130`/`x134` rather than speculative semantic names.
Temporary decomp variables still use register-derived names like `temp_r29`, `var_r31`, and `temp_f31`, indicating partial matching rather than a cleanup/naming PR.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `PAD_STACK(...)` in several functions to satisfy stack layout constraints, including item collision helpers and matrix/JObj display code.
Used `fabs_inline` for small velocity threshold checks in `it_8026DC24`, suggesting inline math was needed for matching floating-point comparisons.

Modeled repeated boolean collision return values precisely by changing `mpColl_8004B2DC` from `void` to `bool` and by returning OR-combined collision tests in helpers like `it_8026DAA8` and `it_8026DFB0`.
Kept explicit casts and bit tests around `u64` masks in item spawning/random-selection code, such as `it_8026D324`, `it_8026CA4C`, and `it_8026CB9C`.
Used local block scoping and temporary variables to manage register pressure and ordering in long functions like `it_8026D018`.
Used inline-like helper comments for repeated matrix translation/view-matrix setup in `it_8026EB18`, noting it likely appears elsewhere in the codebase.
Retained `#if 0` for `it_8026D258`, implying the function had some reverse-engineering progress but was not included as a live matched implementation.
review_feedback: No PR body, issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided slice.
No explicit reviewer feedback or requested changes can be extracted from the available evidence.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or further type `HSD_ObjAllocUnk4`, `HSD_ObjAllocUnk5`, or `HSD_ObjAllocUnk6`.
Find later PRs that complete or remove the `#if 0` implementation of `it_8026D258`.
Search for follow-up fixes to duplicate or conflicting prototypes in `it_266F.h`, especially around `it_8026D604`, `it_8026D62C`, `it_8026E15C`, and `it_8026E248`.
Search for later decomp work that extracts the matrix translation/view-matrix setup in `it_8026EB18` into an inline.
Find later item PRs that use the `itUnkVars` union member or rename `ItemCommonData.x128` through `x134`.
